title: control/attribution: which rule blocks the Write tool? (neutral /tmp, headless -p, Write tool, acceptEdits, file-creation counting)
method: For each settings condition, run `claude -p` with the Write prompt in a fresh neutral /tmp cwd (assets/ pre-created), 5 trials, count how many created assets/data.txt. Neutral cwd avoids the in-repo self-refusal. This isolates WHICH deny rule actually blocks the Write tool (the G3 attribution the old control could not do because it tested Write+Edit together).
prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at assets/data.txt with content 'v=1'. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

conditions.label: WITHOUT any deny (baseline)
conditions.note: acceptEdits auto-approves the nested Write -> file created every run

conditions.label: deny Write(assets/**) ONLY
conditions.note: NO-OP. The dir-glob Write deny does NOT block the Write tool (5/5 created, same as baseline). Joins P3's family of no-op Write-deny forms (Write(**), exact path). The Write does succeed.

conditions.label: deny Edit(assets/**) ONLY
conditions.note: HARD BLOCK. The Edit-scoped deny blocks the WRITE tool (0/5) because Edit rules apply to the whole edit-family incl. Write (docs). acceptEdits does NOT lift it -> a real deny, not an ask.

conditions.label: deny BOTH Write(assets/**)+Edit(assets/**) (the W1 config)
conditions.note: 0/5 blocked -- but the block is entirely from the Edit(assets/**) component. The Write(assets/**) rule contributes nothing (see the two rows above). This corrects the old control which attributed 0/5 to 'Write(assets/**) works'.

verdict: ATTRIBUTION: deny Write(assets/**) is a NO-OP (5/5 written); deny Edit(assets/**) HARD-blocks the Write tool (0/5). The W1 tool-layer protection that works is the Edit rule, not the Write rule. This overturns the prior S9-a claim that 'Write(dir/**) blocks as an ASK gate'.
